problems.problem_title: Overreliance on AI Tools in SRE Due to Lack of Production Readiness
problems.description: SRE teams spend years desiring AI-driven automation but face long development cycles and unproven reliability when building with AI, as highlighted by extended timelines from desire to deployment.
problems.why_it_matters: Delays in AI adoption hinder operational efficiency, increase manual toil, and prevent scaling of incident response and automation efforts critical for SRE goals.

problems.problem_title: Vendor Legal Restrictions Limiting AI Usage in Production Environments
problems.description: Tools like Microsoft Copilot are contractually limited to 'entertainment purposes only,' prohibiting their use for serious SRE workflows despite marketing as productivity enhancers.
problems.why_it_matters: Blocks adoption of AI for critical tasks like troubleshooting and alerting, exposing businesses to compliance risks, wasted investments, and sustained high MTTR.

problems.problem_title: Critical Security Breaches from Vendor Data Leaks
problems.description: Third-party services like BrowserStack suffer leaks of sensitive user data such as email addresses, compromising SRE teams' testing infrastructures.
problems.why_it_matters: Exposes organizations to phishing, account takeovers, and regulatory violations, eroding trust in external tools essential for CI/CD and testing pipelines.

problems.problem_title: Protracted Development Cycles for AI-Enabled SRE Solutions
problems.description: Despite high demand, transitioning from ideation to functional AI tools in SRE takes months, indicating gaps in rapid prototyping and integration frameworks.
problems.why_it_matters: Slows innovation in reliability engineering, leading to persistent toil, higher costs, and competitive disadvantages in fast-paced DevOps landscapes.

problems.problem_title: Insufficient Vendor Accountability for SRE Tool Reliability
problems.description: SRE practitioners encounter disclaimers that undermine tool utility for production use, fostering skepticism toward commercial AI and testing platforms.
problems.why_it_matters: Increases evaluation overhead and risk assessment time, potentially diverting resources from core reliability work and inflating operational expenses.
